articles.title: CONTEXT GRAPHS COULD BECOME AI'S NEXT TRILLION-DOLLAR PLATFORM
articles.summary: AI agents expose a missing enterprise layer: decision traces that capture exceptions, approvals, and precedent that never make it into systems of record. Startups that sit in the agent execution path can persist these traces at decision time, forming context graphs that explain not just what happened, but why it was allowed. This shifts power away from CRMs and data warehouses toward new systems of record built around decision lineage and autonomy.

articles.title: THE COORDINATION TAX
articles.summary: The structural logic for knowledge work is moving toward smaller teams with larger AI support. Companies that recognize this will operate at cost structures their competitors cannot match. Executives' value will lie in judgment, not coordination. The agent built from years of direct context that exists nowhere else is irreplaceable.

articles.title: THIS WEEK ON HOW I AI: HOW TO GET YOUR WHOLE TEAM EXCITED ABOUT AI
articles.summary: Brian Greenbaum, product designer at Pendo, became an AI advocate after a Slack message on paternity leave, leading workshops and implementing tools across teams. His approach includes rapid experimentation with AI tools and offers a playbook for organizational adoption, enhancing creativity and strategy influence. Becoming an AI champion, as Greenbaum emphasizes, significantly boosts career opportunities.

articles.title: THE LAST PRODUCT TO GET CANCELLED
articles.summary: The 'vitamin vs painkiller' metaphor is a prime example of perfect hindsight, non-actionable startup advice. No founder sets out intending to build a vitamin, it's just difficult to come up with actionable tests of what's what. This article presents some concrete tests to help founders create more certainty. The advice is likely most helpful for B2B founders currently navigating product-market fit and early sales.

articles.title: SUPER AGENTS BY CLICKUP
articles.summary: Super Agents by ClickUp are configurable AI agents that can be created, assigned, and managed directly within ClickUp workflows to automate tasks and coordination.

articles.title: /AGENT BY FIRECRAWL
articles.summary: /agent by Firecrawl is an API that autonomously searches, navigates, and extracts structured data from complex websites based on a data description.

articles.title: AIDENT AI
articles.summary: Aident AI is a natural-language automation tool that converts plain English instructions into agent-based workflows and scripts across connected tools.

articles.title: $3B TO ZERO - HOW LUMINAR COLLAPSED
articles.summary: Austin Russell became one of the youngest self-made billionaires when Luminar went public in 2020. By December 2025, the lidar company filed for Chapter 11. This article digs into what happened in between - Luminar bet everything on Volvo, which kept expanding its order from 39,500 sensors to 1.1 million over three years. Then, Volvo made lidar optional instead of standard, cutting volumes by 90%. There's a lot to unpack here about concentration risk, why "lifetime volume" projections aren't the same as purchase orders, and what happens when your only customer changes their mind.

articles.title: THE REAL ECONOMICS OF SAAS VERSUS AI COMPANIES
articles.summary: AI companies need higher revenue or pricing power to match SaaS profit per customer due to structurally lower margins. AI can tap larger budgets, but must rely on volume, pricing power, and efficiencies in costs like compute to be competitive. The math hasn't changed, only the economic inputs have, making understanding these differences crucial for evaluating financial outcomes in AI versus SaaS.

articles.title: THE PRODUCT MODEL AT GOOGLE
articles.summary: Google has more than nine products, each with more than one billion monthly active users, none of which represent new categories.

articles.title: STARTUPS USING AI HAVE A PROBLEM: ANYONE CAN COPY THEIR AWESOME IDEA
articles.summary: Maor Shlomo, founder of the AI startup Base44, warns that 'vibe coding' makes it easy for competitors to quickly replicate ideas.

articles.title: THE SHAPE OF THE GAME WE PLAY
articles.summary: The rapid interest rate hikes in 2022 reduced global liquidity, causing many startups to struggle or fail, highlighting the shift from a low-cost capital environment.

articles.title: WHY $1-10M ARR IS THE HARDEST
articles.summary: Martin Roth, revenue leader at Levelset, breaks down what actually changes as sales teams scale, where teams tend to overcorrect, and why structure and incentives still beat tools, even in the current AI era.
